findings.description: The contact email on page 1 of the PDF is caroline.bari@ncdenr.gov, but on page 2 the contact email is caroline.bari@deq.nc.gov. The HTML preserves both, which matches the PDF. However, the URL in the PDF includes a period as part of the sentence ending ('laserfiche.') while the HTML wraps the period into the hyperlink URL itself, which is a minor formatting issue but not fabrication.
findings.evidence: N/A - withdrawn upon re-examination; both emails exist in the PDF.

findings.description: The document header 'FACT SHEET' that appears at the top of each page in the PDF is not reproduced in the HTML. This is acceptable as header/footer removal.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'FACT SHEET' header on pages 1 and 2. HTML: not present.

findings.description: The URL for the administrative record in the PDF does not include a trailing period as part of the URL, but the HTML anchor tag wraps the trailing period into the href and visible link text.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/waste-management/laserfiche.' (period is sentence punctuation). HTML: https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/waste-management/laserfiche. (period included in URL).

findings.description: The HTML uses 'Draft Corrective Action Permit' as the sole h1 heading. The PDF's actual document title header is 'FACT SHEET' with 'Draft Corrective Action Permit' as a subtitle. While using the subtitle as h1 is an acceptable converter choice, the 'FACT SHEET' title is lost.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'FACT SHEET' followed by 'Draft Corrective Action Permit'. HTML: only h1 is 'Draft Corrective Action Permit'.
